Title: RTD detains three Rohingya men for traffic offences in Kedah
Article:
Two Rohingya men were detained under Op PeWA in Kota Setar, while another was held in Bandar Baharu for various traffic offences, including driving without valid licences. – Source: RTD
ALOR STAR: Three Rohingya men were detained by the Road Transport Department (RTD) in separate operations across Kedah yesterday for various traffic offences, including driving without valid licences.
Two of the men, both in their 40s, were detained under Op PeWA, which targets foreign nationals driving without valid licences, insurance or proper documentation.
They were stopped separately in Kota Setar while on their way to buy daily necessities.
Checks found one of them driving a Perodua Alza, which he claimed had been rented from a Pakistani national, while the other was riding a motorcycle.
Both vehicles had expired motor vehicle licences and were not covered by valid insurance. The vehicles were subsequently seized by Kedah RTD.
Kedah RTD director Stien Van Lutam said such operations would be intensified to ensure compliance with road transport laws and safeguard road users.
In a separate case, another Rohingya man was stopped during an inspection in Serdang, Bandar Baharu.
Stien said the man, a holder of a United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) card, was found riding a motorcycle without a valid licence, riding recklessly and failing to ensure the vehicle was roadworthy.
He said several technical offences were also detected, resulting in summonses being issued under the relevant laws.
The motorcycle was also seized.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 111 investors sue QEW Group, directors for RM20.5 million over failed scheme
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: As many as 111 investors have filed a lawsuit at the High Court against an investment company and its two directors for failing to return their investment capital totalling RM20.5 million.
The oldest investor is an 89-year-old who had invested RM100,000, while the youngest, a 28-year-old, joined the investment scheme with a capital of RM50,000.
The largest single investment among them amounted to RM1.7 million.
All the investors, acting as plaintiffs, claimed that QEW Group Bhd failed to fulfill its responsibilities in managing the investment scheme and began delaying, reducing, and eventually halting dividend payments entirely without any reasonable financial explanation.
Apart from QEW, the plaintiffs also named its two directors, Datuk Dr Muhamad Iqbal Mohamad and Mohd Fairoz Muhammed, as defendants in the lawsuit filed on April 3 through the law firm Messrs. Azri & Co.
In their statement of claim, the plaintiffs alleged that between 2022 and 2024, they were influenced and induced by the defendants to invest in and subscribe to the RPS-i Investment Scheme, which was offered, promoted, marketed, and managed by the defendants.
Before joining the scheme, QEW, through its representatives Muhamad Iqbal and Mohd Fairoz, made material representations regarding the scheme's structure, dividend payment mechanisms, capital preservation guarantees, and compliance with syariah principles.
According to the plaintiffs, the defendants made express or implied representations that the scheme was well-structured, safe, syariah compliant, would yield consistent monthly dividends, and that their investment capital would be secure.
"The defendants also represented that Tawafuq Consultancy Sdn Bhd was appointed as the syariah agent and adviser for the administration and operation of the investment scheme.
"The defendants also said that the investment scheme was not based on the concepts of Musharakah or Mudharabah. Therefore, there were no elements of loss-sharing or risks to be borne under the scheme.
"We state that due to the representations made by the defendants, we agreed to invest in the scheme and made capital payments to the first defendant according to our respective investment amounts," the plaintiffs said.
The plaintiffs added that initially, dividend payments were made as promised.
However, over time, QEW began failing in its obligations when the promised dividend payments were delayed, reduced, and subsequently stopped entirely.
According to the plaintiffs, the defendants said that payments were subject to the absolute discretion of the company—an excuse that was never reasonably explained or highlighted as a key risk when the investment was offered.
The plaintiffs are seeking a court order for the first defendant to pay them RM20.5 million, and a declaration that the second and third defendants acted negligently and executed a fraudulent scheme involving their investment capital.
The plaintiffs are also seeking an order for the second and third defendants to be held jointly and severally liable to indemnify them and pay special damages, also amounting to RM20.5 million, due to negligence, false representation, and fraudulent conspiracy.
Additionally, they are seeking general damages, as well as exemplary and aggravated damages totaling RM1 million from all defendants.
The plaintiffs' lawyer, Dr Hizri Hasshan, said Judicial Commissioner Asmadi Hussin fixed July 6 for a new case management date, after being informed yesterday that the defendants had filed a third-party notice.
The notice aims to include AUF MBZ Consortium PLT as a third-party defendant in the lawsuit.
AUF MBZ is an investment company founded by entrepreneur Datuk Mahadi Badrul Zaman.
On Feb 20, the company, represented by Mahadi, was charged at the Sessions Court here with two counts of carrying on a regulated business of dealing in securities without a license.
Meanwhile, Muhamad Iqbal was charged in his capacity as a director of QEW Group Bhd with abetting AUF MBZ in dealing in securities without a licence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Deadly flare-up in Lebanon rattles US-Iran peace deal
Article:
GENEVA: Deadly exchanges between Israel and Hizbollah in Lebanon put a newly signed deal to end the Middle East war under further strain on Friday, after the postponement of planned US-Iran talks in Switzerland.
The delay came as Teheran's top negotiator warned it would not bend on its red lines and that its finger was still "on the trigger", even as shipping appeared to pick up in the Strait of Hormuz, which had essentially been closed during the war.
The deal signed this week by President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian aims to end a war that began on Feb 28 with US-Israeli strikes that killed supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
The agreement was also meant to halt the fighting in Lebanon, which Iran has always insisted should be covered under any accord, turning Israel's ongoing campaign there into a source of frustration for Washington.
Israel's military said Friday that it had struck more than 80 Hizbollah targets in Lebanon and killed dozens of members of the Iran-backed group in response to what it described as ceasefire violations.
Lebanon said 18 people were killed Friday in Israeli airstrikes in the south, while Israel's military reported four troops were killed, drawing furious reactions at home.
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the Israeli army would stay in Lebanon "as long as necessary" and would make Hizbollah pay a "heavy price" for its attacks.
Far-right Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir went even further, saying after the soldiers' deaths that "all of Lebanon must burn."
France, whose President Emmanuel Macron presided over Trump's signing of the deal in Versailles, urged Israel to "respect" the accord, Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said.
- 'TWO SPOILERS' -
Preparations had been made to host Iranian and US delegations led by Teheran's top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and US Vice President JD Vance at the Swiss resort of Burgenstock, overlooking Lake Lucerne.
The talks were due to kick off a two-month period of negotiations to discuss outstanding issues not covered by the initial deal, notably Iran's nuclear programme.
Switzerland's foreign ministry confirmed the discussions had been postponed but said it "remains ready to facilitate these talks."
Quoting diplomats, the Financial Times said Israel's strikes on Lebanon had led to the postponement but there was no immediate confirmation.
A diplomatic source from key regional player the UAE said there were "two spoilers" to the deal – namely that Israel "didn't like it" and that there was also hardline opposition within Iran.
- 'CRUSHING RESPONSE' -
Ghalibaf said on Friday that talks with the United States would remain bound by Teheran's "red lines."
"If the enemy seeks to be excessive, we have proven that our fingers are on the trigger and we have no hesitation in giving a crushing response to the enemy," he said in remarks published by the official IRNA news agency.
Vance, meanwhile, has expressed a degree of exasperation with the Israeli government rare for a top US official, telling the New York Times "you can't just kill your way out of solving every single national security problem that you have."
Iran's new supreme leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, who succeeded his father, said Thursday that he had approved the accord with the US, despite holding a "different view."
A key aspect of the deal was the immediate re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz, the key shipping bottleneck whose closure caused global energy prices to rise.
A total of 25 commercial vessels crossed the newly-reopened Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, the highest number since mid-April, according to data from maritime tracking firm AXSMarine published on Friday.
A fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas exports passed through the strait in peacetime, according to economists.
American forces on Thursday lifted their naval blockade of Iranian ports that had prevented ships from sailing to or from the Islamic republic, the US military said, noting that American warships "will remain in the general area."
Under the text of the deal, Washington commits to immediately waive oil sanctions crippling Iran's economy.
And once a final agreement is reached on Iran's nuclear programme, the United States will facilitate the release of a US$300 billion reconstruction fund supported by regional nations, the deal says.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: E&O, Cengild and Skyspring team up for RM350mil Penang private hospital
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Eastern & Oriental Bhd (E&O) has partnered with Cengild Medical Bhd and Skyspring Sdn Bhd to develop a multi-disciplinary tertiary private hospital at the Gurney Green district within Andaman Island, Penang.
It will have an estimated development cost of RM350 million, E&O said in a statement today.
The company said its subsidiary KCB Holdings Sdn Bhd had entered into a conditional joint venture agreement with Cengild Medical and Skyspring for the project.
KCB will hold a 30 per cent stake in the joint venture company, while Skyspring and Cengild Medical will own 45 per cent and 25 per cent respectively.
The joint venture company will subsequently establish a wholly-owned operating entity to undertake the development, establishment and operation of the hospital.
The proposed hospital is targeted for completion by the fourth quarter of 2029.
It will be developed in two phases and is expected to have a capacity of about 240 beds.
Cengild Medical is expected to contribute its healthcare expertise, particularly in digestive system and abdominal health and provide clinical, operational and strategic management support for the development and future operations of the facility.
E&O managing director Kok Tuck Cheong said the partnership marks another important milestone in the evolution of Andaman Island.
"As we bring our masterplan to life, we remain focused on creating a well-balanced waterfront township that integrates quality homes with the essential amenities and services needed to support a thriving community," he said in the statement.
"Healthcare is a key component of any sustainable township.  The proposed hospital not only elevates the overall ecosystem of Andaman Island but also strengthens its long-term appeal as a place where families, professionals and retirees can enjoy a high quality of life," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Annuar: Pas never pushed Bersatu out of Perikatan Nasional
Article:
KOTA BARU: Pas has never sought to force Bersatu out of Perikatan Nasional (PN), despite the apparent strain in relations between the two parties.
Pas central committee member Tan Sri Annuar Musa said Pas had never raised the issue of expelling Bersatu from the coalition and was instead focused on addressing the challenges currently facing PN.
"Pas has never spoken about pushing Bersatu out. What is being discussed is how to manage PN amid the current crisis," he told reporters here today.
The PN information chief said all component parties should prioritise unity and work towards resolving differences for the benefit of the coalition.
"If possible, we want peace. If possible, we want unity. And if possible, we want to open PN's doors wider so that more parties can join," he said.
However, Annuar warned that persistent disputes and obstruction within the coalition would hamper its ability to function effectively.
"If everything is blocked, then we cannot move forward. And if we cannot move forward, then perhaps separation becomes unavoidable," he said.
He added that Bersatu's continued participation in PN would not be an issue as long as the party adhered to the coalition's agreed rules and discipline.
Commenting on PN's decision to remove Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali and Datuk Dr Mohd Radzi Md Jidin from key positions within the coalition, Annuar said office-bearers must be able to work closely with all component parties.
"Anyone holding a position in PN must be able to cooperate with all parties within the coalition, not work against them.
"We cannot have leaders who are constantly in confrontation with other components. When that happens, it affects cooperation and prevents the coalition from moving forward.
"If the situation persists, changes may have to be made," he said.
Annuar likened the situation to a marriage that could no longer be repaired despite repeated attempts at reconciliation.
"What choices do we have? Tell me, what options are left if a husband and wife continue to quarrel and cannot resolve their differences?
"At some point, separation may become the only option, not because it is good for one side, but because it may be the best solution for both parties," he said.
PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar recently announced that
Azmin and Radzi had been removed
from their positions in the coalition's latest leadership reshuffle.
Samsuri said Azmin was removed as deputy secretary-general, while Radzi was relieved of his duties as election director-general.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hormuz ship traffic climbs after war deal: trackers
Article:
PARIS: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz rose to its highest rate in two months after a deal to halt the US-Iran war, maritime trackers said today.
A total of 25 commercial vessels crossed the newly reopened strait on Thursday, the highest number since mid-April, according to data from tracking firm AXSMarine.
The spike came after Iran and the United States agreed this week to re-open the crucial route under an agreement to end the war, but before the postponement of talks between the sides in Switzerland that had been planned for today under that deal.
On June 18 "we observed 25 verified commercial vessel crossings through the Strait of Hormuz – the highest single-day count since 18 April and more than five times the average daily level recorded during the first ten days of June", AXSMarine said in a news release.
Iranian forces effectively closed off the strait after US and Israeli strikes sparked the war on Feb 28. Maritime authorities reported dozens of attacks on ships in the area.
Iran later briefly re-opened the global trade artery to commercial traffic, prompting a short peak in crossings on April 18.
Before the war, about 120 vessels a day passed through the strait, according to shipping journal Lloyd's List.
A fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas exports passed through the strait in peacetime, according to economists.
AXSMarine said crossings averaged 7.6 a day from the start of March.
The number of crossings on Thursday may be higher, as some ships turn off or manipulate their AIS transponder signals to avoid detection while passing through the strait.
Thursday's "spike came amid the largest AIS signal disruption event we have observed in the Persian Gulf since the conflict began, with more than 200 commercial vessels affected simultaneously by spoofing or abnormal AIS behaviour", it said.
- Safe shipping plans -
The signing of the agreement to stop the war this week was also meant to halt fighting in Lebanon, but Israel's military announced today new strikes there.
Meanwhile, shipping groups warned this week that plans to resume traffic through the strait were still not clear and it was not thought safe to start exiting the Gulf through.
Jakob Larsen, chief security officer at shipping lobby BIMCO, said however that the body "expects an international coordination body to be established shortly to facilitate transits."
International Maritime Organization (IMO) chief Arsenio Dominguez said in April that the body was working on a plan to ensure safe transit for ships out of the Gulf.
More than 500 commercial vessels and about 11,000 seafarers are still stuck in the Gulf, according to the IMO. It says 20,000 seafarers in the region have been affected by the war overall.
The closure of the strait during the war drove up global oil prices and choked off shipments of energy and crucial commodities such as fertiliser.
Following the Iran-US agreement announced on June 14, "the first sign of relief came this week with fast falling prices", said Ipek Ozkardeskaya, a senior analyst at banking group Swissquote.
"Energy and transport sectors will be the first to feel the relief, before it spills toward the rest of the economy," she told AFP.
"But questions remain regarding the US ability to end the war without Israel's willingness to participate." - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MyCC, DOSM ink agreement to strengthen data sharing, economic analysis
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) and the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) have signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) to strengthen strategic cooperation in data sharing, economic analysis and capacity building.
The MoU was signed by MyCC chairman Tan Sri Idrus Harun and Chief Statistician of Malaysia Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin at a ceremony held at the DOSM headquarters here yesterday.
The signing was witnessed by MyCC chief executive officer Datuk Iskandar Ismail and DOSM Deputy Chief Statistician (Economic Programmes) Siti Asiah Ahmad.
MyCC said in a statement that the collaboration signifies the government's commitment to adopt a data-centric approach to strengthen the implementation of government policies, including enforcement efforts related to competition issues in the country.
According to the commission, the collaboration provides a framework that encompasses the strengthening and sharing of data, capacity building programmes and the exchange of expertise to continuously monitor and improve competition processes across various sectors of the economy.
MyCC also views the collaboration as an opportunity to strengthen the role of data and analytics in competition economics, especially when data is now considered the world's main commodity.
Meanwhile, Mohd Uzir said in a separate statement that the MoU covers the sharing of administrative and related economic data, as well as enabling a more comprehensive evaluation and understanding of data through combining expertise in the fields of statistics and competition.
He said the collaboration also focuses on the development of human capital through capacity-building programmes, training, exchanges of expertise and knowledge-sharing initiatives between the two organisations.
"This strategic collaboration also focuses on joint monitoring of strategic economic sectors and government policies to ensure the implementation of fair competition across all economic sectors," he said,
Mohd Uzir said the collaboration is expected to strengthen the capablities of both agencies in supporting the provision of more comprehensive statistics and analyses, in addition to enhancing understanding of market structures and dynamics, supply chains and factors influencing price movements.
He said that, overall, the MoU will strengthen the sharing of knowledge and expertise between both agencies, as well as support the development of a more competitive and transparent economic ecosystem, while helping to safeguard the interests of businesses, consumers and the Malaysian economy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nhaveen murder case: Federal Court strikes out prosecution appeal
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court today struck out the prosecution's notice of appeal against the acquittal of a man charged with murdering bullying victim T. Nhaveen nine years ago.
A three‑member bench led by Datuk Nordin Hassan made the ruling after deputy public prosecutor Allan Suman Pillai informed the court that the notice could not be served personally on S. Gopinaath, 35, who resides in Penang, as he could not be located.
"This is the fifth time the prosecution has attempted to serve the notice of appeal. I seek further directions from the court," said Allan.
Also on the bench were Datuk Seri Vazeer Alam Mydin Meera and Datuk Azimah Omar.
The prosecution had appealed against the Court of Appeal's decision on Jan 13, 2025, to acquit Gopinaath of murdering Nhaveen, then 18, at a park along Jalan Bunga Raya between 11pm and midnight on June 9, 2017.
Gopinaath was charged in 2021, four years after the incident, while the prosecution's seventh witness was testifying.
The Court of Appeal also ordered four men – J. Ragesuthan and S. Gokulan, both now 27, and two others who were juveniles at the time – to enter their defence on a murder charge after allowing the prosecution's appeal against the Penang High Court's Oct 3, 2023 decision to discharge and acquit them.
The appellate court remitted the case to the Penang High Court for the four men to enter their defence. They were also ordered to defend themselves against a charge of causing injury to Nhaveen's friend, T. Previin, near the Karpal Singh Learning Centre along Jalan Kaki Bukit, Gelugor, between 11pm and midnight on June 9, 2017.
Case management has been fixed for July 3 at the Penang High Court to set trial dates.
Nhaveen sustained serious injuries after being assaulted by a group of youths in Gelugor. He was admitted to Penang Hospital but died six days later. His friend Previin was also assaulted with motorcycle helmets but managed to escape and seek help.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Queen: Mutual respect among foundations of harmony upheld by Malaysians
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mutual respect is among the foundations of harmony that Malaysians have always upheld, Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, said.
Her Majesty said it has also contributed to the nation's unity and well-being.
In a post on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar today, Raja Zarith Sofiah stressed the importance of being courteous and fostering mutual respect in society, both at home and abroad.
"Her Majesty reminded particularly the Muslims in the country of a hadith narrated by Al-Bukhari," the post said.
The hadith was narrated by Abu Hurairah RA, in which Prophet Muhammad said: "Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should not harm his neighbour. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should honour his guest. And whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day must either speak good or remain silent."
Recalling an occasion in February, the Queen warmly interacted with more than 100 tourists from various countries at the main entrance of Istana Negara.
"This reflects Her Majesty's graciousness and concern for the people and visitors to the country," the post added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Investors may face a trickier second half after resilient first half: Standard Chartered
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Markets have remained resilient in the first half of 2026, supported by strong corporate earnings and continued optimism surrounding artificial intelligence (AI).
Standard Chartered global chief investment officer Steve Brice, however, said investors are likely to face a more complex environment in the second half of the year.
Brice outlined aeveral factors that could influence market sentiment and direction.
"In this environment, staying invested, maintaining diversification and being prepared to take advantage of periods of volatility will be key to capturing opportunities.
"Our latest Global Market Outlook is designed to help clients remain focused on these priorities and navigate the shifting landscape ahead," he said in a statement.
Standard Chartered's Wealth Solutions chief investment office (CIO) has released its Global Market Outlook for the second half of 2026, outlining its investment strategy and key themes for investors navigating an evolving market landscape.
The CIO expects risk assets to remain supported by a soft-landing macroeconomic environment despite heightened volatility stemming from energy prices, equity supply, investor positioning and central bank policy.
Against this backdrop, the bank remains overweight on global equities, particularly in the US and Asia excluding Japan, while also identifying selective opportunities in fixed income and alternative investments.
The CIO has set a target of 7,950 for the S&P 500 Index and US$5,100 for gold by mid-2027, reflecting its positive outlook on equities as a growth driver and gold as a strategic portfolio diversifier.
Global equities have gained more than 12 per cent year-to-date.
This was supported by strong earnings growth and AI-driven optimism despite geopolitical tensions, higher oil prices and elevated bond yields.
While the momentum is expected to continue into the second half, the CIO said investors will need to navigate four key factors.
These include energy prices, where an interim US-Iran peace deal could help ease supply constraints and soften oil prices, although risks remain due to the temporary nature of the agreement and potential delays in restoring production.
The bank also pointed to a strong US initial public offering (IPO) pipeline, which may increase near-term equity supply, although recent successful listings indicate investor demand remains resilient.
In addition, optimistic investor positioning could raise the risk of short-term market pullbacks, which may present opportunities for investors to add exposure.
On monetary policy, the CIO said easing energy prices could reduce pressure on central banks to tighten policy.
But resilient labour markets may limit the scope for interest rate cuts, particularly in the US.
The Federal Reserve is expected to keep rates unchanged through the end of 2026.
Overall, the CIO continues to expect a soft-landing scenario for the global economy, while noting a smaller possibility of stronger growth re-acceleration.
Although recession and stagflation risks remain, the bank said softer energy prices have reduced the likelihood of these outcomes.
Within fixed income, the CIO favours emerging market US dollar-denominated bonds as current yields remain attractive for income generation, while gold continues to be its preferred portfolio diversifier alongside alternative investment strategies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bukit Bintang to get new mosque by early next year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A new mosque capable of accommodating more than 1,500 worshippers will be built in Bukit Bintang and is expected to be completed early next year, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan.
He said the mosque will replace the Outreach Oasis Bukit Bintang (OOBB) community centre, currently used as a temporary Friday prayer surau, following approval by the Federal Territories Syariah Law Consultative Committee on June 8.
"Once completed, all congregants will gather there, while the OOBB community centre will continue with its dakwah activities," he told reporters after performing Friday prayers at OOBB today.
Zulkifli said the mosque's design and plans have been prepared, with construction expected to begin soon.
"The project is being carried out with the cooperation of various parties, including the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (JAWI), the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP), and several non‑governmental organisations," he added.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gaza ceasefire a 'deadly illusion': UNICEF
Article:
GENEVA: The ceasefire declared in Gaza more than eight months ago is a "deadly illusion", the UN charged today, with 265 children killed there since the fighting was supposed to have stopped.
Despite a ceasefire being declared in October 2025, Israel has continued to launch strikes across Gaza, killing at least 992 Palestinians since then, according to the occupied territory's health ministry, whose figures are considered reliable by the United Nations.
The UN children's agency said the number of Palestinian children killed since the ceasefire was announced between Israel and Hamas, which runs Gaza, was an "absurd and devastating figure."
"During a period supposedly defined by restraint and protection, a child has been killed, on average, every single day for more than eight months," UNICEF spokesman James Elder told reporters in Geneva, speaking from Amman.
"For many, many months, the world has been told there is a ceasefire in Gaza. Yet for Palestinian children, this so-called ceasefire has become a cruel and a deadly illusion."
Elder stressed that the children killed since the ceasefire was declared "were not killed in a war zone."
"They were killed in their homes. In their schools. Playing football. Fishing. They were shot, bombed struck by quadcopters," he said.
"If a child is being killed every day, surely the debate is no longer about the quality of the ceasefire. It is about the credibility of calling it one."
This week, he pointed out, "a two-year-old boy was shot and killed by Israeli forces; a 13-year-old boy was shot and killed inside his tent; a five-year-old boy and his father were killed by an Israeli strike, and on and on it goes."
- Unacceptable -
In addition to those killed, more than 400 children had been injured since the ceasefire was declared, "many with catastrophic wounds", Elder said.
Currently, he said, "hundreds of children urgently require medical evacuation", even as Israeli "restrictions on essential medicines mean wounded children are enduring greater pain and face an increased risk of infection, complications and further amputations."
Elder also highlighted the deep trauma suffered by Gaza's children.
"Fear, loss and violence... is woven into the very fabric of their childhood," he said, pointing out that "the trauma is so profound that it affects children's ability to eat, sleep and, of course, to develop normally."
Elder insisted: "The continued killing of children is not the consequence of a lack of options. It is the consequence of a lack of political will."
"We must stop accepting levels of child deaths that would provoke international outrage anywhere else in the world," he said.
"We must stop normalising the abnormal." - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three Zahid media officers escape serious injury in Maran accident
Article:
MARAN: Three media officers attached to Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi were among seven people injured in a crash involving three vehicles at Km144 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) early today.
The 1am accident involved a Toyota Fortuner carrying four occupants — a driver and three media officers — and two lorries.
Maran police chief Superintendent Wong Kim Wai said all three vehicles were travelling towards Kuala Lumpur when the crash occurred.
"Investigations revealed that one of the lorries is believed to have suddenly changed lanes from the left lane into the overtaking lane.
"The Fortuner driver was unable to avoid the vehicle and collided with the right side of the lorry.
"A Hicom lorry travelling in the same lane attempted to avoid the collision and swerved before crashing into the metal road divider," he said in a statement today.
Wong said all seven victims, including three occupants of the two lorries, sustained minor injuries and were taken to Jengka Hospital for treatment.
He said the case is being investigated under Rule 6(1) of the Road Traffic Rules 1959 (LN 166/59) for allegedly overtaking in a dangerous manner, an offence punishable by a fine of up to RM2,000, imprisonment of up to six months, or both.
Wong urged witnesses to come forward to assist in the investigation by contacting investigating officer Sergeant Mohd Junaidi Jamat at 019-4544864 or the nearest police station.
Earlier, Zahid, who attended a programme in Kuantan late yesterday, said in a Facebook post that his media officers were involved in an accident near Maran while travelling home after attending the Pahang State Huffaz Gathering 2026 at Yayasan Pahang.
"Alhamdulillah, they are all safe and did not suffer serious injuries. Thank God for His protection and mercy.
"To all those on duty, take good care of yourselves. Get enough rest, drive carefully and always prioritise safety, no matter how busy you are.
"May those involved recover fully soon and continue to be protected by Allah," he wrote.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two men freed from drug trafficking charges after 5 years behind bars
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A former lorry driver was acquitted by the High Court today after spending five years in detention on drug trafficking charges.
Shafie Yakob, 40, said he delivered a package for an acquaintance and had no reason to suspect it contained illegal substances.
"I will never again blindly agree to transport items for others.
"I was only doing them a favour and never suspected it contained drugs.
"I was arrested and jointly charged with a man I do not even know at all, for allegedly trafficking drugs," he said.
Shafie said the years behind bars were especially painful as he lost both his father and a sibling during his detention and was unable to see them for the last time.
"It was tragedy after tragedy. But today I have been vindicated. I just want to start over and go into a small business," he said.
He was released after High Court judge Datuk Seri Latifah Mohd Tahar, at the end of the prosecution case, acquitted him and co-accused welder Mohd Sofian Tomingan, 48, of trafficking 141,147.6g of cannabis.
The pair had been charged on two joint counts involving 94,402.9g and 46,744.7g of cannabis, allegedly committed at 6.15am at Subang Industrial Park in Subang Jaya on Feb 1, 2021.
The charges were framed under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act, punishable under Section 39B(2), read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code for common intention.
The offence carries the death penalty or a life term and not less than 12 strokes of the rotan if found guilty.
Sofian said the years of detention had taken a heavy toll as he was separated from his family.
"The truth has prevailed and I am relieved. After five years, four months and 19 days, I can finally return to my family," he said.
Latifah, who has since been elevated to the Court of Appeal, acquitted and discharged both men after ruling that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case.
She said although it was not disputed that the seized substances were drugs, the prosecution failed to prove control, custody and knowledge by the accused.
She added that temporary possession was insufficient to establish the control and custody elements and because the prosecution failed to prove direct possession, the presumption of trafficking could not be invoked.
"The court finds that a substantial amount of drugs is not the sole factor to prove trafficking.
"The court also finds that the presence of the accused together alone cannot automatically establish the existence of the element of common intention.
"There must be sufficient statements and evidence, whether oral or otherwise, pointing to a pre-arranged plan between the first and second accused.
"After a maximum evaluation of the testimony of prosecution witnesses, the court finds that the prosecution has failed to prove a prima facie case against the accused.
"The accused are acquitted and discharged from the charge without being called to enter their defence," she said.
Both men broke down in tears upon hearing the verdict.
Shafie's sister, Rohani, 44, who was in the public gallery, was also seen crying.
Deputy public prosecutor Mohammed Heikal Ismail led the prosecution.
Sofian was represented by lawyers Rahmat Hazlan, Mohamed Yazzer Azzad Zainudin and Ahmad Nadim, while Shafie was represented by T. Santhakumari.
The trial, which began in August last year, saw six prosecution witnesses testify.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melaka eyes new flights to Batam, Kota Baru and Penang
Article:
MELAKA: Three new routes, one international and two domestic, are expected to open via Melaka International Airport (LTAM), Batu Berendam, by year‑end.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh said the proposed routes are Melaka–Batam (Indonesia), Melaka–Kota Baru and Melaka–Penang.
"The state government is negotiating with several airlines, including a low‑cost carrier, to open the three routes. We intend to realise them this year, subject to airline assessments," he told reporters after visiting LTAM in conjunction with the Melaka–Pekanbaru–Melaka flight today.
Also present were deputy exco for Tourism, Heritage, Arts and Culture Datuk Zaidi Attan and Melaka Tourism Promotion Division general manager Nasrul Ahmad.
Ab Rauf said the state is consulting the federal government to ensure LTAM facilities meet operational needs.
He added the new routes are part of efforts to promote Melaka as a tourist destination, boost the sector and strengthen the state economy.
"Although Melaka is small, we have 150 flights a day operated by the Malaysia Flying Academy. The opening of new routes will further increase air traffic and highlight Melaka's potential as an aviation hub," he said.
Earlier, he said the visit to LTAM was aimed at reviewing improvements to facilities, including Immigration services and tourist arrivals.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trading of Ajinomoto's shares to be suspended on Monday pending announcement
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Trading of Ajinomoto (Malaysia) Bhd's (AMB) shares will be suspended from 9am to 5pm on Monday pending an announcement.
"Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd has approved AMB's request to suspend the trading of its ordinary shares with effect from 9am to 5pm on Monday, June 22, 2026, pending the release of a material announcement.
"The request for suspension is made under subparagraph 3.1(b)(i) of Practice Note 2 on Requests for Suspension of the Main Market Listing Requirements of Bursa Securities," AMB said in an announcement on Bursa Malaysia today.
At close, the counter fell 16 sen or 1.04 per cent to RM15.20, with 37,000 shares transacted.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'No news without journalists': Fahmi rejects proposal for AI-generated news reports
Article:
SEBERANG JAYA: The government remains committed to ensuring journalists are not marginalised as artificial intelligence (AI) reshapes the media landscape, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today.
Speaking at a gathering with Gabungan Kelab Media Malaysia (GKMM) and representatives of state media clubs, Fahmi stressed that journalists continue to play an irreplaceable role in society despite rapid technological advances.
"Without journalists, there is no news. Journalists are vital because they witness events firsthand, gather information from primary sources and apply professional judgment and experience to produce credible reporting," he said.
Fahmi revealed that the ministry had previously rejected a proposal to develop an AI application capable of automatically generating news reports from official statements, saying such initiatives risk undermining the profession.
"I cannot support efforts like that. To me, journalists are too important to be replaced by AI," he said.
The minister also urged media organisations to avoid workforce reduction measures, including rightsizing exercises and mutual separation schemes, following the government's introduction of the Media Innovation Fund to support the industry's sustainability.
He called on GKMM to alert the ministry immediately if any media company was facing financial difficulties that could lead to job losses.
"If there are media companies facing challenges to the extent that they are considering such measures, please inform me immediately so we can explore possible solutions," he said.
Fahmi said the government's commitment to the media industry was reflected in the establishment of the Malaysian Media Council, noting that much of the legislation was drafted based on feedback from media practitioners.
He added that closer engagement between policymakers and industry players would remain crucial in addressing emerging issues, including ethical reporting practices and the growing impact of AI on journalism.
"We need continuous discussions on issues such as reporting on suicide and the challenges posed by AI.
"These are matters that require collective thinking and collaboration to determine the best path forward for the country," he said.
Earlier, Fahmi announced financial contributions of RM10,000 to each state media club affiliated with GKMM and RM30,000 to the council, while expressing hope that more media organisations and practitioners would join the umbrella body.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to launch National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 here tomorrow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Medical, insurance, takaful practitioners boost healthcare ecosystem via HPPSC platform
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Five medical and insurance/takaful associations said initiatives are being implemented to enhance communication and operational efficiency among doctors, hospitals, insurers and takaful operators.
The five are the Malaysian Medical Association (MMA), the Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia, Life Insurance Association of Malaysia (LIAM), Malaysian Takaful Association, and Persatuan Insurans Am Malaysia.
In a joint statement today, they said the initiatives are to improve the overall customer experience across the healthcare ecosystem.
This will be done via the Healthcare Partners Protocol & Solutions Committee (HPPSC) platform.
The four broad categories of initiatives include improving communication between medical practitioners and insurers, takaful operators; enhancing transparency in panelling and review processes for cashless facility; improving medical and health insurance / takaful claims management operational efficiency; and streamlining the guarantee letter issuance process.
The statememt said a dedicated "doctor-to-doctor" hotline was set up by insurers and takaful operators in April 30, 2026, to support and address claim enquiries and enable direct discussions between medical practitioners and insurers and takaful operators' medical advisory teams.
LIAM chief executive officer Mark O'Dell said broadly, the initiatives are ultimately designed to benefit patients and policyholders.
"As healthcare needs continue to evolve, it is important that insurers, takaful operators, healthcare providers and regulators work collaboratively to develop practical and sustainable solutions that support timely access to care while ensuring the long-term affordability and sustainability of medical and health insurance and takaful coverage for Malaysians," he said.
MMA's Dr Gunalan Palari said effective patient care depends on strong collaboration and mutual understanding among all parties involved in the healthcare journey.
"Through HPPSC, we have established a constructive platform that enables healthcare providers and healthcare financiers to address operational challenges, improve communication and develop solutions that support better clinical and patient outcomes.
"The implementation of initiatives such as the 'doctor-to-doctor' hotline demonstrates our collective commitment to ensuring patients receive timely, appropriate and coordinated care," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas, Turkmenistan strategic partnership opens new chapter in bilateral economic, energy relations
Article:
ASHGABAT: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov today witnessed the signing of a strategic partnership between Petronas and Turkmenistan authorities, marking a new chapter in economic and energy relations between the two countries.
In a statement today, the Prime Minister's Office (PMO) said the agreement coincides with 30 years of Malaysia-Turkmenistan cooperation in the energy sector, which has been a cornerstone of bilateral ties.
"This relationship not only strengthens the friendship between Malaysia and Turkmenistan, but also contributes to economic growth, talent development, and the transfer of knowledge and technology that benefits both parties.
"The partnership also opens up opportunities for more strategic involvement in developing future energy resources, including exploring the vast potential of Turkmenistan's natural gas sector, which holds some of the world's largest gas reserves," the PMO said.
The strategic partnership also reflects international confidence in Malaysia's capabilities in the energy sector and strategic industries.
The PMO noted that it demonstrates the country's expertise, talent and technology can compete on the global stage and contribute to economic development and energy security worldwide.
"The Madani government welcomes this development, which not only strengthens Malaysia-Turkmenistan bilateral relations but also creates broader economic and investment cooperation opportunities in the future," it said.
Earlier, Anwar, who is on a two-day official visit that began yesterday, was received by Berdimuhamedov at the official welcome ceremony at the Presidential Palace before leading the Malaysian delegation to a bilateral meeting with Turkmenistan.
In 2025, Turkmenistan was Malaysia's fourth-largest trading partner among the Central Asian countries.
The bilateral trade between Malaysia and Turkmenistan amounted to RM75.80 million, with Malaysia's exports valued at RM75.50 million, marking a nine per cent increase from the previous year.
Turkmenistan is also a key investment destination for Petronas, with the company's total investments in the country reaching RM52.73 billion since 1996.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit closes lower against greenback amid continued expectation for Fed rate hike
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Friday as persistent expectations of a possible US Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate hike later this year continued to drive the greenback.
At 6pm, the local note fell to 4.1340/1395 against the greenback from 4.1145/1195 at Thursday's close.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the prospect of higher US interest rates supported the greenback, exerting pressure on Asian currencies, including the ringgit.
"(Asian) currency markets continued to suffer from fears of monetary tightening by the Fed, where it is expected to increase the federal funds rate by 25 basis points this year," he told Bernama.
At the close, the ringgit was lower against a basket of major currencies.
It dipped against the Japanese yen to 2.5636/5671 from 2.5588/5620 at Thursday's close and weakened against the British pound to 5.4709/4782 from 5.4476/4542 previously. It also slipped versus the euro to 4.7376/7439 from 4.7222/7280.
The local note was also lower against regional currencies.
It weakened against the Singapore dollar to 3.2019/2064 from 3.1898/1939 and declined against the Thai baht to 12.5856/6074 from 12.5611/5809 at Thursday's close.
The ringgit also depreciated versus the Indonesian rupiah to 232.2/232.5 from 231.2/231.5 previously and eased against the Philippine peso to 6.80/6.81 from 6.79/6.80.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Lost wallet row over alleged RM10,000 goes viral
Article:
SEMENYIH: A man who found a lost wallet at a supermarket was confronted by its owners over an alleged RM10,000 in missing cash, with videos of the incident later going viral on social media.
The video, uploaded today by TikTok user "C J", showed a couple, believed to be Chinese nationals, demanding that the man return the money allegedly kept inside the wallet.
The man repeatedly denied taking any cash inside the wallet.
In a subsequent video, the wallet owners said the wallet had been left behind at the supermarket's freezer section after one of them stepped away to answer an urgent phone call.
They claimed CCTV footage showed a child picking up the wallet and handing it to his mother before the family left the premises.
Police later identified the family through a vehicle registration number from the CCTV recording, after which the man went to a police station in Semenyih and handed over the wallet.
However, the woman seen taking the wallet in the footage did not accompany him to the police station.
In the video, the wallet owners demanded the woman to show up to the police station to help with the investigation.
The TikTok video has garnered more than 270,000 views and attracted mixed reactions online, with some users questioning the claim that RM10,000 had been inside the wallet.
"If it was withdrawn from the bank, where is the evidence?" user Mummy Triple R wrote.
"The CCTV does not prove the existence of the cash as it only identifies the movement of the wallet from one person to another," user Raja Kapoor wrote.
Others argued that the man should not be accused without proof.
"In this case, I can't see any solid proof that he committed any foul play. It would be better not to tarnish a person's good name by uploading this," user misso95 wrote.
Some users also questioned why the wallet had not been handed over to supermarket staff or taken directly to a police station.
"But what's wrong is that his wife took the wallet and put it in her bag. Why didn't she go directly to the counter and tell the cashier? I don't understand," user Nuryanah wrote.
Kajang police later confirmed they received a report on the incident on Wednesday night and arrested the man who found the wallet.
The suspect was subsequently remanded for four days to assist in the investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Delivery rider arrested for allegedly taking RM10,000 from wallet lost at supermarket
Article:
KAJANG: A 37-year-old delivery rider has been arrested for allegedly stealing RM10,000 in cash from a lost wallet at a supermarket in Semenyih.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said police received a report on Wednesday night.
The theft is believed to have taken place at 3pm at the premises.
Police later arrested the man at 1am on Thursday.
The arrest follows a
viral video
showing a woman confronting the delivery rider after he handed back her lost wallet.
In the clip, the woman claims that the RM10,000 cash originally inside the wallet was missing and accuses him of taking it, which the rider repeatedly denies.
"The suspect has been remanded for four days to assist in investigations," Naazron said in a statement.
The case is being investigated under Section 380 of the Penal Code for theft in a building, which carries a jail term of up to 10 years and a fine upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Businesses should identify pain points amid rising uncertainty – Economist
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian businesses should identify their operational pain points before making strategic decisions as the business landscape becomes increasingly uncertain, said AmBank Group chief economist Firdaos Rosli.
He said companies can no longer operate the way they did in the past, as geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions and rapidly evolving risks require businesses to become more resilient and agile.
"The first thing companies need to understand is where their pain points are. Doing business today is no longer the same as before, and every day brings new risks that businesses need to be aware of," he said during the 'AmBank BizDialogue Market Outlook Series: Middle East in Focus' session, held virtually today.
Against this backdrop, Firdaos said Malaysia's economy is expected to remain resilient, although growth would likely moderate over the coming quarters while inflation gradually rises amid higher energy prices and supply chain disruptions.
"We expect growth to come down moderately over the next couple of quarters, while inflation starts to rise as a result of this.
"Unemployment will probably increase a little bit but not to a dangerous level, while loan growth appears to remain quite stable," he said.
In the first quarter of 2026, Malaysia's economy grew at 5.4 per cent year-on-year, while headline inflation averaged 1.6 per cent and the unemployment rate remained at 3.0 per cent,
Meanwhile, Firdaos said the balance of risks remains tilted to the downside, with the Strait of Hormuz continuing to pose one of the biggest external risks to Malaysia, as any prolonged disruption could drive up energy prices, operating costs and imported inflation.
He urged businesses to work closely with banks, not merely as financing providers but as strategic partners that could help companies assess risks, strengthen resilience and identify opportunities for sustainable growth.
Firdaos said feedback gathered by banks from businesses is also channelled to Bank Negara Malaysia, enabling policymakers and the government to better understand industry challenges and formulate appropriate support measures.
Echoing the call for businesses to become more adaptive, integrated plastic recycling company, Heng Hiap Industries Sdn Bhd (HHI) founder and chief executive officer Seah Kian Hoe said companies should also develop contingency plans for their supply chains and continuously reassess their business models to manage growing uncertainties.
He said businesses have traditionally operated in a relatively stable environment where efficiency and scale were the primary focus, but today's increasingly dynamic landscape requires companies to become more flexible and responsive.
"We have to reassess our business model and move from a fixed model to a dynamic model," he said.
He also said that manufacturers should adopt dynamic manufacturing and pricing strategies that enable them to respond quickly to changing customer demand, rather than focusing solely on operational efficiency.
At the same time, Seah said uncertainties should also be viewed as an opportunity to create value.
For example, during periods of supply constraints, he said HHI focused on offering supply availability while strengthening product certification and sustainability credentials, positioning recycled materials as a value-added solution instead of merely a cost-saving alternative.
He also said that businesses must continue investing in long-term capabilities while managing immediate challenges.
"We have to fight the war while building the city," he said.
Seah also called for greater localisation of supply chains by working more closely with local manufacturers and recyclers to strengthen domestic industrial capabilities and reduce dependence on imported materials.
He added that businesses should collaborate more closely with industry associations and government agencies, while accelerating investments in renewable energy solutions such as solar power and battery energy storage systems to improve long-term resilience and competitiveness.
At the regional level, Seah said closer Asean cooperation would be increasingly important in strengthening regional supply chains and enabling businesses to better withstand future disruptions while capturing new growth opportunities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MDEC appoints Ganesh Kumar as non-executive chairman
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) has appointed prominent technology entrepreneur and MOL Global Inc founder Ganesh Kumar Bangah as its non-executive chairman.
In a statement today, the agency said Ganesh's appointment comes at a critical time as the country moves beyond digital adoption towards deeper execution in artificial intelligence, digital investments, small and medium enterprises (SMEs) digitalisation, talent development, GovTech innovation and the growth of local technology champions.
Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said Ganesh's appointment is expected to further strengthen MDEC's ability to align government priorities with industry execution, given his decades of experience building technology companies, scaling digital platforms and working with entrepreneurs, SMEs and the wider technology ecosystem.
"Ganesh's appointment reflects the government's commitment to ensuring that MDEC continues to be guided by strong industry experience and expertise as Malaysia enters our nation's next phase of digital economy growth.
"This experience will be valuable as we continue to translate Malaysia's digital economy priorities into outcomes that support investment, innovation, job creation and wider participation by businesses and local talent," Gobind said.
Ganesh, who has been an MDEC board member since February 2025, is also a current board member of Payments Network Malaysia (PayNet) and previously served as chairman of the National Tech Association of Malaysia (PIKOM) and a board member of the Malaysian Global Innovation and Creativity Centre (MaGIC).
Regarded as one of Malaysia's most established technology entrepreneurs, Ganesh has built and scaled digital platforms across online payments, e-commerce, social commerce and the creator economy, among others.
Meanwhile, MDEC chief executive officer Anuar Fariz Fadzil said Ganesh's appointment would add significant value to MDEC's work in ensuring that the agency remains relevant, agile, responsive and closely aligned with the needs of Malaysia's technology industry.
"Ganesh's vast experience in building technology companies, supporting entrepreneurs and working across the ecosystem will be valuable in helping us strengthen that industry alignment," said Anuar.
He added that MDEC would remain focused on translating Malaysia's digital economy priorities into tangible outcomes for industry, SMEs, talent and local technology companies, while ensuring that growth remains anchored in inclusivity, innovation and Malaysia's continued position as a trusted digital hub in the region.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's vehicle sales hit reverse gear in May
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's new vehicle sales, or total industry volume (TIV), fell 15 per cent to 61,250 units in May from 72,113 units in April, ending two straight months of growth.
The Malaysian Automotive Association (MAA) attributed this mainly to the long festive holiday period, encompassing Hari Raya Aidiladha, Pesta Kaamatan and Hari Gawai.
Production fell by 13 per cent year-on-year to 57,489 units in May 2026, compared to 66,354 units in May 2025.
For the January-May period, MAA said sales inched down one per cent to 315,568 units from 319,846 units in the corresponding period last year.
Production fell four per cent to 287,477 units from 300,559 units previously.
Looking ahead, MAA said it expects sales to regain momentum this month.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CIMB launches regional campaign to drive social progress
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd (CIMB) has unveiled its new regional brand campaign, 'Moving You Forward', reinforcing its commitment to enabling customers, businesses and communities across Asean to achieve meaningful progress.
Anchored in the group's purpose of advancing customers and society, the campaign marks the next evolution of CIMB's brand journey and reflects its belief that the true value of banking lies in what it enables.
CIMB's chief brand and marketing officer Toni Darusman said the group's ambition extended beyond banking.
"There is a clear vision that guides everything we do, and that is to be the bank that advances the ambitions of people across Asean.
"It is rooted in the belief that real, meaningful progress happens when business and society move forward together," he said.
He said 'Moving You Forward' reflects how CIMB is bringing its purpose of advancing customers and society to life in a more meaningful and impactful way.
The campaign will be rolled out in phases across social media, digital out-of-home and out-of-home platforms in key regional markets.
It will include a brand film, wall art installations at MRT stations, as well as on-ground and social activations.
RECOGNISING COMMUNITY EFFORTS
The launch coincided with CIMB's inaugural 'CIMB Advancing Societies 2026 – Recognising Impactful Partners' initative, which honoured 11 non-governmental organisations, social activists and community partners from Malaysia, Singapore, the Philippines and Indonesia.
The honourees were recognised for their efforts in uplifting underserved communities, improving livelihoods, advancing education and protecting the environment.
National track cyclist Datuk Azizulhasni Awang, who is also a CIMB brand ambassador, received the bank's 'Kita Bagi Jadi Hero' recognition for his inspiring journey and continued dedication to empowering others beyond the sporting arena.
CIMB Foundation and CIMB Islamic chief executive officer Ahmad Shahriman Mohd Shariff said meaningful progress should be measured through the positive changes experienced by communities.
"Real progress is not defined by programmes or numbers alone. It is seen in how lives have improved, opportunities are created and the confidence communities gain to move forward," he said.
He added that CIMB's commitment to advancing customers and society included a pledge of RM200 million from 2026 to 2030 to support community initiatives across Asean.
"Our approach focuses on enabling individuals and communities to initiate and lead initiatives that are sustainable in the long term, building the capability and resilience needed for them to thrive independently.
"Through CIMB Advancing Societies 2026, we are proud to celebrate these individuals and organisations whose efforts are creating meaningful impact on the ground," he said.
Among the Malaysian organisations recognised were Ong Yong Xun, the founder of JomStudy, an educational platform, and a scholarship recipient of Yayasan Tunku Abdul Rahman; Thrive Well's Safe Space Programme, which provides community-based mental health support; and WWF Malaysia, which works to protect the Ulu Muda Forest Complex and other critical ecosystems.
Other honourees included Indonesia's Berdaya Bareng Foundation, Kehati Foundation and Sinergi Mahakarya; the Philippines' Empower and Transform and Pave Philippines; as well as Singapore's Lions Befrienders and Halogen Singapore.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: George Kent expands water ops with Techkem acquisition
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: George Kent (Malaysia) Bhd is buying a 76 per cent stake in Techkem Group Sdn Bhd for RM2.0 million in cash, strengthening its position in the water and wastewater treatment sector.
Techkem specialises in potable water treatment, industrial wastewater treatment, sewerage treatment, wastewater recycling and reclamation, specialty chemicals and sustainable engineering solutions.
It provides end-to-end services spanning engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning, consultancy, operations and maintenance, as well as design, build, operate and transfer projects.
It also distributes specialty chemical products for industrial and municipal applications.
Over the past decade, Techkem has completed more than 65 water and wastewater treatment installations across the region and developed customised treatment solutions to meet increasingly stringent environmental and sustainability requirements.
Among its recent projects is a build-operate-transfer potable water treatment plant with an operational capacity of about 4,000 cubic metres per day under a concession running from 2025 to 2030.
Techkem is also developing a backwash water reclaim plant with a treatment capacity of 3,000 cubic metres per day, which is expected to be among the first facilities in Malaysia dedicated to washwater recovery and reuse.
As at May 31, 2026, Techkem had an outstanding order book of RM106.5 million and a tender book of approximately RM1.5 billion.
George Kent said the acquisition is expected to enhance its order book and tender pipeline.
The proposed acquisition will be funded through internally generated funds and is expected to be completed in the third quarter of 2026.
George Kent executive chairman Tan Sri Tan Kay Hock said the acquisition represents a strategic expansion of its water infrastructure capabilities and reinforces the long-term commitment towards strengthening its presence within the sector.
"We believe the acquisition is timely given the growing demand for sustainable water
infrastructure solutions and the long-term opportunities arising from Malaysia's Water
Sector Transformation 2040 agenda.
"George Kent will emerge from this acquisition as a more integrated and competitive player within the regional water and wastewater industry," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vestland unit secures RM65mil works contract
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Construction company Vestland Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Vestland Infra Sdn Bhd (VISB) has accepted a letter of award from Scanwolf Trading & Construction Sdn Bhd (STCSB) for a works contract worth RM65 million.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, Vestland said the contract involves piling and substructure works for one block of 74-storey service apartment, including car parks, basement, shop lots, and related facilities at Jalan Pavilion here.
"The completion of the contract works shall be on June 18, 2028, with the commencement date today," it said.
The company also said the contract will have no effect on the issued share capital and substantial shareholders' shareholdings of Vestland as there is no issuance of new ordinary shares in the company.
"The contract is expected to contribute positively to the earnings and net assets of Vestland and its subsidiaries for the duration of the contract assuming no material delay in the timing," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RoS confirms PN leadership transition, activities require chairman's consent
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Any activity or meeting conducted under PN must obtain the approval of its chairman, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar.
PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said this follows the Registrar of Societies' (RoS) acknowledgement of its receipt of the minutes of PN's extraordinary Supreme Council meeting held on Feb 22.
He said the RoS, in an official response dated today, acknowledged, among other matters, the Supreme Council's acceptance of the resignation of PN chairman Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin and the appointment of Ahmad Samsuri as his successor.
"RoS also confirmed receipt of the minutes of the Supreme Council Meeting (No. 1/2026) dated March 14, as well as the list of office-bearers and appointments to the new PN Supreme Council committee, for its records," he said in a statement today.
Takiyuddin said PN remained committed to ensuring that all party activities, administrative matters and management affairs were conducted in accordance with its constitution and in compliance with the provisions of the Societies Act 1966.
On Dec 30, Muhyiddin confirmed his
resignation
as PN chairman, effective Jan 1.
On Feb 22, Samsuri, who is Pas vice-president and Terengganu Menteri Besar, was appointed PN
chairman
following the coalition's extraordinary Supreme Council meeting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Storm tears through Ipoh, damages over 200 homes in Anjung Bercham (VIDEO)
Article:
IPOH, June 19 — A violent afternoon storm ripped through Anjung Bercham here, damaging more than 200 homes across seven residential areas and triggering emergency response teams on the ground.
The Kinta District Civil Defence Force Operations Control Centre said the incident struck at about 3.47pm after reports were relayed by the Ulu Kinta (Tanjung Rambutan) village head,
Bernama
reported this evening.
“Acting on the information, three APM personnel were deployed to monitor the affected areas,” it said.
Among the affected locations were:
Dataran Tasek Timur 6 (177 houses)
Hala Tasek Timur 1 (22 houses)
Lorong Bercham 19 (22 houses)
Hala Tasek Timur 34 (10 houses)
Tasek Timur 15 (five houses)
Lintasan Tasek Timur 1 (two houses)
Lintasan Tasek Timur 3 (two houses)
The Ipoh City Council (MBI) deployed emergency teams at 5.05pm to clear fallen trees and debris blocking roads and posing risks to residents.
The storm also brought down several electricity poles, forcing TNB to temporarily shut off power supply in affected areas as a precaution.
Dewan Datuk Ahmad Said has been designated as the temporary evacuation centre.
The Kinta district office has directed all agencies to remain on alert following more than an hour of continuous heavy rain.
Locals have taken to social media to share pictures and videos of the storm and its aftermath.
https://www.facebook.com/61564218535232/videos/ribut-di-ipoh-rosakkan-belasan-bumbung-rumah-di-taman-bercham-idaman19-jun-2026-/1321070366219588/

Title: JB nasi kandar shop suffers RM200,000 losses in pre-opening paint and arson attack
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — Police are hunting two masked men wearing helmets who are believed to have splashed fuel and red paint before setting fire to a nasi kandar restaurant in Bandar Dato’ Onn here early this morning, a day before the premises was scheduled to hold its official opening tomorrow.
Johor Bahru South district police chief ACP Raub Selamat said police received a report regarding the incident at 3.59 am from a 30-year-old local man who operates the restaurant and based on the restaurant’s closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, the two suspects arrived at about 3.34 am before carrying out the act.
“The incident resulted in losses estimated at about RM200,000 and the cause of the incident is still unknown,” he said in a statement today.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 436 of the Penal Code and urged members of the public with information related to the incident to contact the Johor Bahru South District Police Headquarters hotline at 07-2182323.
Meanwhile, Johor Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 3.43 am and dispatched personnel from the Tebrau Fire and Rescue Station and Kempas Fire and Rescue Station to the scene.
However, the fire was successfully extinguished before the arrival of the fire and rescue team using eight fire extinguishers, and no injuries were reported.
The restaurant had previously announced its official opening tomorrow and was offering free food to visitors in conjunction with the event. — Bernama

Title: PAS takeover complete as ROS confirms new Perikatan leadership with Samsuri as chair
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) has officially consolidated its leadership under Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar following formal confirmation from the Registrar of Societies (ROS).
With the transition now legally recognised, PAS secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan warned today that all meetings and activities held under the party banner must have the chairman’s explicit approval to remain in compliance with the Societies Act.
The directive follows the receipt of an official ROS letter dated today and addressed to Takiyuddin in his capacity as secretary-general of the coalition; the correspondence solidifies the coalition’s administrative standing and settles its management structure.
The ROS validation centres on two pivotal developments. First, the department has formally acknowledged the minutes from PN’s extraordinary supreme council meeting held on February 22, 2026.
This document records the supreme council’s formal acceptance of the previous chairman’s resignation and the official appointment of his successor.
Second, the ROS has officially logged the minutes from the supreme council meeting dated March 14, 2026. This filing includes the full roster of leadership appointments and the specific composition of the new PN Supreme Council committee, ensuring these records are now part of the national registry.
“In this regard, Perikatan Nasional remains committed to ensuring that all activities, administrative matters, and party management are carried out in accordance with the party's constitution and in compliance with Act 832.
“Therefore, it is emphasised that no activity or meeting using the name Perikatan Nasional may be carried out except with the consent of the chairman of Perikatan Nasional, Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar,” he said in a statement.
The move officially puts PAS in full control of the coalition following the split with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia.

Title: Lorry lane change triggers East Coast pile-up near Maran involving Zahid’s aides and driver
Article:
KUANTAN, June 19 — Seven individuals, including a driver and three media officers to Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, were involved in a three-vehicle accident at Kilometer 144.2 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) near Maran early this morning.
Maran District Police Chief Supt Wong Kim Wai said preliminary investigations revealed that the accident occurred around 1:00 am when a Foton lorry allegedly changed lanes abruptly causing the driver of a Toyota Fortuner heading towards Kuala Lumpur to lose control when trying to avoid but and resulting in a collision with the right side of the lorry.
“Another vehicle, a Hicom lorry, which was behind the two vehicles, tried to avoid the accident but lost control before crashing into the road divider,” he said in a statement today.
As a result of the accident, he said seven people traveling in the three vehicles sustained minor injuries and were sent to the Jengka Hospital for treatment.
Wong added that the incident is being investigated under Rule 6 (1) of the Road Traffic Rules 1959.
Meanwhile, Ahmad Zahid, through a Facebook post, informed that all those involved in the accident were on their way back after attending the 2026 Pahang State Huffaz Gathering at Yayasan Pahang here last night.
He also expressed his gratitude to Allah SWT that all of them were safe and did not suffer serious injuries.
“To everyone currently on duty, please take good care of yourselves. Get enough rest, drive carefully, and always prioritise safety no matter how busy you are.
“May those involved make a full and speedy recovery, and may we all always be protected by Allah SWT wherever we are,” he said. — Bernama

Title: MetMalaysia says rough weather for KL and 10 states until 7pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit 10 states and Kuala Lumpur until 7 pm today.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), in a statement issued today, said the warning covers several areas in Kedah, namely Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Pendang, Sik and Baling.
In Perak, the affected areas are Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim.
Similar weather conditions are forecast in Kelantan (covering Kota Bharu, Bachok, Machang and Pasir Puteh), as well as in Terengganu (Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang and Dungun).
In Pahang, the warning applies to Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Temerloh and Bera.
In Selangor, the areas affected are Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat, while in Johor, it involves the areas of Segamat, Kluang and Mersing.
The same warning is also issued for Sarawak involving the areas of Betong, Sarikei (Pakan, Sarikei and Meradong), Sibu (Sibu) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis, Daro and Matu) and Sabah involving the Interior (Tambunan), West Coast and Kudat. — Bernama

Title: Use your own logo and leave: Bersatu info chief says PAS never truly committed to Perikatan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz today said that PAS should leave Perikatan Nasional (PN) and form a new coalition under its own logo and brand, instead of remaining in the opposition pact.
In a Facebook post today, Tun Faisal said Bersatu should remain in PN with other component parties and its allies under Ikatan Prihatin Rakyat.
“From the beginning, I have held the view that instead of continuously trying to force PAS’ will in a way that affects the spirit of comradeship, consensus and PN’s interests, it would be more appropriate for PAS to go solo, or form Muafakat Nasional with Umno as its leaders often repeat, or form a new coalition party with Parti Wawasan,” he said.
Tun Faisal claimed PAS had not been fully committed to PN, citing what he described as political negotiations outside the coalition’s framework.
He also cited PAS leaders’ repeated interest in reviving Muafakat Nasional with Umno, and allegations that the Islamist party had held meetings with government leaders without Bersatu or PN’s knowledge.
Tun Faisal also claimed PAS had not been loyal to the PN brand, pointing to its use of the PAS logo in Kelantan and Terengganu and earlier suggestions that it could contest in Johor under its own logo.
He said PAS had weakened PN’s identity and appeal by making the coalition appear more like a PAS-led “green” bloc than a broader “blue” coalition that could attract non-Malay parties.
His remarks come after Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said the party would remain in PN and contest the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections using the coalition’s logo.

Title: After five failed attempts, prosecution bid to revive Penang teen Nhaveen’s murder case ends at Federal Court
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 19 — The Federal Court today struck out the prosecution’s appeal against the acquittal of a man on a charge of murdering bullying victim T. Nhaveen nine years ago.
A three-member Federal Court bench led by Datuk Nordin Hassan made the ruling after Deputy Public Prosecutor Allan Suman Pillai informed the court that the notice of appeal could not be served personally on S. Gopinaath, 35, who resides in Penang, because the respondent could not be located.
“This is the fifth time the prosecution has attempted to serve the notice of appeal. I seek further directions from the court,” said Allan.
Also sitting on the bench were Datuk Seri Vazeer Alam Mydin Meera and Datuk Azimah Omar.
The prosecution had appealed against the Court of Appeal’s decision on Jan 13, 2025, to acquit Gopinaath of murdering Nhaveen, who was 18 at the time, at a park along Jalan Bunga Raya between 11pm and midnight on June 9, 2017.
